result.stdout:
Description:
  Various operations for NGINX config files.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  parse <filename>   Parse an NGINX config file to a JSON payload.
  build <filename>   Build NGINX config files from a JSON payload.
  lex <filename>     Tokenise an NGINX config file to a JSON array.
  minify <filename>  Remove all whitespace from an NGINX config.
  format <filename>  Format an NGINX config file.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Various operations for NGINX config files.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  parse <filename>   Parse an NGINX config file to a JSON payload.
  build <filename>   Build NGINX config files from a JSON payload.
  lex <filename>     Tokenise an NGINX config file to a JSON array.
  minify <filename>  Remove all whitespace from an NGINX config.
  format <filename>  Format an NGINX config file.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Build NGINX config files from a JSON payload.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool build <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The JSON payload file.

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print paths of written files.
  -d, --dir <d>     Base directory to build in.
  -f, --force       Overwrite existing files without prompting.
  -i, --indent <i>  Spaces per indent level. [default: 4]
  -t, --tabs        Indent with tabs instead of spaces.
  --no-headers      Do not write the header comment to configs.
  --stdout          Write configs to stdout instead of files.
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Build NGINX config files from a JSON payload.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool build <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The JSON payload file.

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print paths of written files.
  -d, --dir <d>     Base directory to build in.
  -f, --force       Overwrite existing files without prompting.
  -i, --indent <i>  Spaces per indent level. [default: 4]
  -t, --tabs        Indent with tabs instead of spaces.
  --no-headers      Do not write the header comment to configs.
  --stdout          Write configs to stdout instead of files.
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Format an NGINX config file.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool format <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The NGINX config file.

Options:
  -o, --out <o>     Write output to a file.
  -i, --indent <i>  Spaces per indent level. [default: 4]
  -t, --tabs        Indent with tabs instead of spaces.
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Tokenise an NGINX config file to a JSON array.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool lex <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The NGINX config file.

Options:
  -o, --out <o>       Write output to a file.
  -i, --indent <i>    Number of spaces to indent output.
  -n, --line-numbers  Include line numbers in JSON.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Parse an NGINX config file to a JSON payload.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool parse <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The NGINX config file.

Options:
  -o, --out <o>       Write output to a file.
  -i, --indent <i>    Number of spaces to indent output.
  --ignore <ignore>   Ignore directives (comma-separated).
  --no-catch          Stop after the first error.
  --combine           Flatten includes into one single config entry.
  --single-file       Do not follow include directives.
  --include-comments  Include comments in JSON output.
  --strict            Raise errors for unknown directives.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Parse an NGINX config file to a JSON payload.

Usage:
  CrossplaneSharp.Tool parse <filename> [options]

Arguments:
  <filename>  The NGINX config file.

Options:
  -o, --out <o>       Write output to a file.
  -i, --indent <i>    Number of spaces to indent output.
  --ignore <ignore>   Ignore directives (comma-separated).
  --no-catch          Stop after the first error.
  --combine           Flatten includes into one single config entry.
  --single-file       Do not follow include directives.
  --include-comments  Include comments in JSON output.
  --strict            Raise errors for unknown directives.
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information
